youtube_data.search_query: Gran Fondo Panama gran fondo cycling Panama City

videos.title: Gran Fondo Panama: An Epic Ride through the Rainforest
videos.description:
The Ocean to Ocean Gran Fondo is a legendary mass start event that traverses the Isthmus of Panama going from the Atlantic Ocean to the Pacific.
Being born in Panama, this was an emotional adventure for me to partake in... not to mention the physical challenge of the 68 miles and 3500 feet of elevation gain. 
In this video I share the raw ride footage I captured as well as some of thoughts along the way. I hope you enjoy it.

#granfondo #panama #rainforest

Website: https://woto.ink/bMV7nq
Discount Code: Luis10
#powermeter #magene #riding  

00:00 Introduction



__________________________________


*Visit Joe's No Flats
www.joes-no-flats.com

*TGW MERCH STORE: 
https://linktr.ee/thegoodcyclist

https://www.amazon.com/shop/the_good_cyclist

Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/luisomarscott

rvplou@yahoo.com
defiantcycleworks@gmail.com

videos.curation_reason: Excellent first-person race recap with raw ride footage, personal emotional connection, and detailed course description including specific distances and elevation.

videos.title: Gran Fondo Océano a Océano Panamá 2019
Is Panama the BEST Kept Secret for Cycling Enthusiasts?
videos.description:
I got the most out of my recent trip to Panama, by incorporating an amazing trip to San Blas Island, before taking to the bike for the 2025 Gran Fondo Cycling Challenge, Ciudad de Panama.

Discount Code: LuisGFCC25
GRAN FONDO CYCLING CHALLENGE MIAMI 2025 Online Registration https://share.google/7IfbgaJ8FE5g1C4jF


#bassobikes #GFCC #cycling

00:00 Introduction
00:34 Arrival to Panama
01:50 Visiting San Blas Day Trip
04:00 Island Hoping
06:46 Exploring The Mainland
08:40 Cycling Prep and Event Fondo
09:30 Gran Fondo Cycling Footage

that one off of the bucket list. So,
we're going to be running that same
event, but not in Panama, in Miami on
November 9th. It's called the Grand
Fondo Cycling Challenge, Sudad de Miami.
I did it last year. I'm doing it again
this year. In fact, I'm one of the event
ambassadors this year. And I have a
registration link that I'll post in the
description below. Uh, November is when
we're really starting to get into the
sweet spot of our weather, and that is a
fantastic time for you to come down with
your bike and enjoy all of the cultural
aspects of Miami. It is going to be lit.
You definitely do not want to miss that
one. So, make sure you check out the
website and start planning to be down
here with us November 9th. My wife and I
spent the rest of the time exploring,
enjoying the amenities of the hotel, and
enjoying Panama's food culture. We
really had a good time doing that. But
it was just every time I go back home, I
have a blast. So, it was a despite the
disappointment of not being able to
finish up the event, we had a fantastic
time. All right, so that's where I'm
going to leave it. I appreciate you guys
indulging by watching this entire
epic video. It was really like a labor
of love for me to be able to do this and
I appreciate you guys taking the time to
watch it and to continue to support the
channel the way how you guys have been
with your subscriptions, your comments,
and your likes. All right, guys. As
always, our aim here is to inform,
instruct, inspire. Be blessed.
videos.curation_reason: Recent 2025 race experience with travel context and cycling preparation footage from the same experienced creator.

videos.title: Travel Vlog for Epic Bike Ride Across Panama Rain Forest
videos.description:
Traveling to Panama for the Ocean to Ocean Gran Fondo and exploring some of the local cuisine and tourist hot spots was a fun experience. Join us for day 1 and 2 of our weeklong discovery of the nation of my birth.

#granfondo #panama #rainforest

Website: https://woto.ink/bMV7nq
Discount Code: Luis10
#powermeter #magene #riding  

00:00 Introduction



__________________________________


*Visit Joe's No Flats
www.joes-no-flats.com

https://linktr.ee/thegoodcyclist

https://www.amazon.com/shop/the_good_cyclist

*LET'S CONNECT:
Instagram:  https://www.instagram.com/the_good_cyclist/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/luisomarscott

*CYCLING CONSULTATION AND MORE:
rvplou@yahoo.com
defiantcycleworks@gmail.com

Disclaimer: Some links may be affiliate links, I may receive compensation for items you purchase through my store, no extra expense to you.(thanks for supporting me by using my links!)

videos.curation_reason: Complementary travel and preparation content providing valuable context for race participants planning their trip to Panama.

videos.title: 2015 Grand Fondo Panama, Panama
videos.description: Ride From Paitilla, Panama to Nitro City, Panama. May 2015. 100 km.

quotes.text: The ocean to Ocean Grand Fondo boasts about 5,000 participants from over 20 different countries representing the Americas Europe Asia and the Caribbean. It's a total of about 68 miles and we covered about 3,600 ft of elevation gain we traversed the canal a total of four different times going over two of the newer Bridges one of the more traditional Bridges.

Anyone who has ever participated in a major massart event or a Fondo knows what this is It's that roll walk sachche type thing to where you can actually sit and pedal. The roads are a little bit wet but it looks like the rain is going to hold for us to be able to get through the ride.

key_challenges.name: Atlantic Bridge
key_challenges.description: 5% grade crossing that gets climbed twice in the early miles. Roads can be damp and riders frequently pull over with flats in this section.

key_challenges.name: Canal Crossings
key_challenges.description: Four total canal traversals including double crossing of Atlantic Bridge, single crossing of Puente Centenario, and single crossing of Puente de las Americas.

terrain_notes.text: Roads can be damp during the ride. Early sections see a lot of cyclists pulled over nursing flats.
Major mass-start events create roll-walk-stop type conditions where you can barely sit and pedal in early miles.

race_day_tips.text: Scheduled pedals up at 6:30 but expect delays for dignitary introductions.
Early going features roll-walk-stop mass start conditions - expect to barely be able to sit and pedal initially.

additional_quotes.text: Getting through customs was a breeze. From the airport, we opted for a private taxi into the city, which was about $35 for us and the bike box. Thanks to the recent highway known as El Corridor, even in rush hour, this was about a half-hour drive.

The event organizer selected three hotels to play host to cyclist traveling into Panama. We selected this Marriott Studio Suite as our home away from home. It's just off via Espana and within walking distance of local restaurants and the ride headquarters.

rider_intel.search_text: The Gran Fondo Panama Ocean to Ocean event covers 68 miles from the Atlantic to Pacific Ocean with 3,600 feet of elevation gain, featuring four canal crossings including a challenging double crossing of the Atlantic Bridge with 5% grades at mile 7. The race attracts 5,000 participants from over 20 countries. Early sections experience mass-start roll-walk-stop conditions where riders can barely pedal. Roads can be damp and many cyclists pull over with flats in the opening miles. The route traverses multiple bridges including Puente Centenario and Puente de las Americas. Start time is scheduled for 6:30 AM but expect delays for opening ceremonies. Airport transfers cost around $35 via private taxi, with multiple host hotels available within walking distance of race headquarters and local restaurants.
